{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warehouse Personnel",
  "description": "Romkol Global Services is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Personnel

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We arelooking for a reliable and hardworking Warehouse Personnel to support warehouse operations, stock management, and inventory control.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive supplied stock and confirm quantity and condition
	Issue stock to sales representatives and customers accurately
	Monitor stock levels and help prevent stock loss
	Maintain proper warehouse documentation and records
	Support effective inventory and warehouse management operations.


Requirements


	B.Sc or HND qualification
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience
	Knowledge of Microsoft Excel is compulsory
	Strong documentation and analytical skills
	Ability to work effectively under pressure with minimal supervision
	Candidates willing to learn and undergo training are also encouraged to apply.


Work Schedule:


	Monday &ndash; Saturday - 8:00 AM &ndash; 6:00 PM.


Reporting Line:


	The Warehouse Personnel will report directly to the Business Manager and work closely with the Auditor, Accountant, Inventory Officers, and Warehouse Staff.


Salary
N80,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hirexus360@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Warehouse Personnel Job at Romkol Global Services




        
    

        
        Romkol Global Services is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Personnel

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We arelooking for a reliable and hardworking Warehouse Personnel to support warehouse operations, stock management, and inventory control.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Receive supplied stock and confirm quantity and condition
	Issue stock to sales representatives and customers accurately
	Monitor stock levels and help prevent stock loss
	Maintain proper warehouse documentation and records
	Support effective inventory and warehouse management operations.


Requirements


	B.Sc or HND qualification
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience
	Knowledge of Microsoft Excel is compulsory
	Strong documentation and analytical skills
	Ability to work effectively under pressure with minimal supervision
	Candidates willing to learn and undergo training are also encouraged to apply.


Work Schedule:


	Monday – Saturday - 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM.


Reporting Line:


	The Warehouse Personnel will report directly to the Business Manager and work closely with the Auditor, Accountant, Inventory Officers, and Warehouse Staff.


Salary
N80,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Payroll Officer",
  "description": "PPC (formerly Philips Projects Centre) is a wholly Nigerian-owned multi-competency engineering and infrastructure development company. Its core activities are primarily geared towards providing specialized end-to-end services for projects in ICT, Power, Healthcare, Transportation, Building Services and other utility sectors.

Commencing operations in 1991 as Philips Projects Centre, an affiliate of Philips Electronics N.V. of the Netherlands. The PPC acronym was retained after a strategic management buyout of the Dutch parent company. Thus, with this strategic buyout, the old businesses were retained, with the added advantage of the exclusive distributorship of the numerous professional Philips&#39; products, and the provision of integrated solutions based on cutting-edge technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Payroll Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Process monthly payroll accurately and on time for all employees, including full-time, part-time, and contract staff.
	Collect, verify, and reconcile attendance, overtime, and leave records from HR and line managers before payroll runs.
	Calculate and process statutory deductions, including PAYE (Personal Income Tax), pension contributions, National Housing Fund (NHF), and other applicable levies.
	Ensure timely remittance of all statutory deductions to relevant government agencies (PENCOM, NHF, NSITF, etc.) within stipulated deadlines.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date payroll records, employee salary data, and deduction schedules in the HRIS or payroll software.
	Prepare and distribute pay-slips to all employees every pay cycle.
	Handle payroll queries from employees professionally and resolve discrepancies promptly.
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual payroll reports for management and finance review.
	Support internal and external payroll audits by providing accurate records and documentation.
	Monitor and implement updates to payroll processes in response to changes in tax laws, pension regulations, and minimum wage legislation.
	Coordinate with HR on new hires, exits, promotions, salary adjustments, and other changes that affect payroll.
	Ensure strict confidentiality of all payroll information and employee financial data.
	Assist in the preparation of annual tax returns and other statutory filings related to employee compensation.
	Continuously identify opportunities to improve payroll processes and controls.


Job Specifications


	Education: Bachelor&#39;s degree in Accounting, Finance, Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional Qualification: Professional certification in HR or payroll management is an added advantage (e.g., CIPM, ICAN, ACCA, SHRM).


Experience:


	Minimum 4 years of hands-on payroll processing experience.
	Experience working with payroll software.
	Demonstrable experience handling statutory remittances (PAYE, pension, NHF).
	Experience preparing payroll reports and reconciling with general ledger.


Key Competencies Requirement:


	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, pivot tables, data analysis).
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and tax regulations (PITA, Pension Reform Act).
	Understanding of payroll accounting principles.
	Knowledge of statutory deduction calculations and filing processes.
	Familiarity with HR information systems and data management.
	Ability to generate and interpret payroll reports and financial data.
	Understanding of data protection principles as they relate to employee information (NDPA compliance.


Behavioral:


	Collaborative team player with a proactive attitude.
	High Emotional Intelligence.
	High level of integrity and ethical conduct.
	Methodical and process-oriented approach to work.
	Self-motivated with minimal need for supervision.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: hr@ppcng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-09",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-17",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "PPC Limited"
    
  },

Payroll Officer Job at PPC Limited




        
    

        
        PPC (formerly Philips Projects Centre) is a wholly Nigerian-owned multi-competency engineering and infrastructure development company. Its core activities are primarily geared towards providing specialized end-to-end services for projects in ICT, Power, Healthcare, Transportation, Building Services and other utility sectors.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Commencing operations in 1991 as Philips Projects Centre, an affiliate of Philips Electronics N.V. of the Netherlands. The PPC acronym was retained after a strategic management buyout of the Dutch parent company. Thus, with this strategic buyout, the old businesses were retained, with the added advantage of the exclusive distributorship of the numerous professional Philips' products, and the provision of integrated solutions based on cutting-edge technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Payroll Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Process monthly payroll accurately and on time for all employees, including full-time, part-time, and contract staff.
	Collect, verify, and reconcile attendance, overtime, and leave records from HR and line managers before payroll runs.
	Calculate and process statutory deductions, including PAYE (Personal Income Tax), pension contributions, National Housing Fund (NHF), and other applicable levies.
	Ensure timely remittance of all statutory deductions to relevant government agencies (PENCOM, NHF, NSITF, etc.) within stipulated deadlines.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date payroll records, employee salary data, and deduction schedules in the HRIS or payroll software.
	Prepare and distribute pay-slips to all employees every pay cycle.
	Handle payroll queries from employees professionally and resolve discrepancies promptly.
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual payroll reports for management and finance review.
	Support internal and external payroll audits by providing accurate records and documentation.
	Monitor and implement updates to payroll processes in response to changes in tax laws, pension regulations, and minimum wage legislation.
	Coordinate with HR on new hires, exits, promotions, salary adjustments, and other changes that affect payroll.
	Ensure strict confidentiality of all payroll information and employee financial data.
	Assist in the preparation of annual tax returns and other statutory filings related to employee compensation.
	Continuously identify opportunities to improve payroll processes and controls.


Job Specifications


	Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional Qualification: Professional certification in HR or payroll management is an added advantage (e.g., CIPM, ICAN, ACCA, SHRM).


Experience:


	Minimum 4 years of hands-on payroll processing experience.
	Experience working with payroll software.
	Demonstrable experience handling statutory remittances (PAYE, pension, NHF).
	Experience preparing payroll reports and reconciling with general ledger.


Key Competencies Requirement:


	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, pivot tables, data analysis).
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and tax regulations (PITA, Pension Reform Act).
	Understanding of payroll accounting principles.
	Knowledge of statutory deduction calculations and filing processes.
	Familiarity with HR information systems and data management.
	Ability to generate and interpret payroll reports and financial data.
	Understanding of data protection principles as they relate to employee information (NDPA compliance.


Behavioral:


	Collaborative team player with a proactive attitude.
	High Emotional Intelligence.
	High level of integrity and ethical conduct.
	Methodical and process-oriented approach to work.
	Self-motivated with minimal need for supervision.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

	Develop, update, and manage all GMYT websites including e-commerce, corporate, and landing pages

Website Developer & Manager Job at GMYT Group Limited




        
    

        
        GMYT Group Limited - We’re committed to equipping aspiring fashion entrepreneurs with practical skills and business knowledge to thrive in the fashion industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Website Developer & Manager
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking a skilled Website Developer & Manager to build, maintain, and optimize our digital platforms, ensuring seamless performance across all GMYT websites.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop, update, and manage all GMYT websites including e-commerce, corporate, and landing pages
	Ensure websites are responsive, fast, secure, and SEO-optimized
	Manage CMS platforms such as WordPress, Shopify, and custom-built sites
	Implement updates, plugins, security patches, and backups regularly
	Troubleshoot technical issues and ensure 99%+ uptime
	Collaborate with Marketing, ICT, and Branding teams to deploy new features and campaigns
	Monitor analytics, site performance, and user experience to drive continuous improvement
	Maintain domain, hosting, and SSL certificate renewals


Requirements


	Minimum 3 years of experience in web development and management
	Proven experience as a Website Developer / Manager with portfolio of live sites
	Strong proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, and at least one modern framework
	Hands-on experience with WordPress, Shopify, and other CMS platforms
	Knowledge of SEO best practices, Google Analytics, and performance optimization tools
	Familiarity with web hosting, cPanel, Git, and basic server management
	Problem-solving mindset, attention to detail, and ability to meet deadlines


What We Offer


	Competitive salary based on experience
	Opportunity to lead GMYT’s digital presence across multiple brands
	Collaborative and growth-oriented work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV, portfolio links, and brief note on your web management experience to: [email protected] using "Application – Website Developer & Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
